vulnerability.title: Configure SSH Server to Use FIPS 140-2 Validated MACs: opensshserver.config
vulnerability.description: Crypto Policies provide a centralized control over crypto algorithms usage of many packages. OpenSSH is supported by system crypto policy, but the OpenSSH configuration may be set up incorrectly. To check that Crypto Policies settings are configured correctly, ensure that /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config contains the following text and is not commented out: -oMACS=
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Configure SSH Server to Use FIPS 140-2 Validated MACs: opensshserver.config

triage_decision.reason: The fix only edits a crypto‑policy backend file that influences SSH MAC algorithms. This is a standard STIG hardening step and does not require reboot or kernel changes. While an incorrect MAC list could prevent SSH logins, the change is low‑risk if reviewed and applied carefully.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; SSH client compatibility issues; Potential SSH login failure if MAC list is mis‑configured
